text: Most businesses don't have an AI problem. They have an implementation problem.
A real estate company in NYC was spending hours every two weeks manually searching StreetEasy
The AI use cases getting the most attention right now are mostly wrong for most businesses.
Six months ago I started building AI tools for clients. Three months ago I realized I needed one

summary: Ops process is either automated or someone's part-time job
$1,500 construction dashboard replaced weekly PM email chain for Aya
Hardest part of AI implementation is getting old process to actually stop

hook_type: Behind the scenes — personal AI OS
summary: 8 months of Eve running 24/7 — real insight was about logistics not AI
Ops process automated or by hand — which is it?

hook_type: Earned lesson — discovered cost the hard way
summary: Isolated cron sessions don't inherit model defaults — $0.57 Opus run
$1,500 construction dashboard replaced weekly email chain
Boring automation (auto-updating spreadsheet) worth more than demo chatbot
Workflow fails when nobody changed the routine around it
StreetEasy scraper: n8n + Python + Google Sheets, runs every 14 days untouched

hook_type: Compressed personal insight — logistics vs. thinking
summary: 6 months Eve 24/7 — real insight: automation returns time from logistics
